title: Equipment for applying drugs simultaneous to electroporation during electrochemotherapy procedure, drug and electroporation applicator device, method of preparing applicator device and method of calculating and adjusting electric current for electroporation
abstract: The present invention equipment (1) for applying drugs in living tissues performed simultaneously with the electroporation of tissues during an electrochemotherapy procedure, the equipment (1) comprising an applicator device (121) of drugs and electroporation. Furthermore, the present invention refers to a method of preparing the applicator device (121) for its use in the equipment (1) and to a method of calculating and adjusting an electric current for electroporation.
claims:
Claims 1. Equipment (1) for applying drugs simultaneously to electroporation, characterized in that it is formed by a first module (11 ) generator, calibrator and electric pulse trigger and a second gauge module (12) of electrical resistance of living tissues in real time and applicator of drugs and electrical pulses. 2. The equipment (1) according to claim 1 , characterized in that the first module (11) comprises: i) an electrical pulse generator (111), ii) a voltage calibrator module (1 12); and iii) a trigger device (113), and the second module (12) comprises an applicator device (121). 3. An applicator device (121) of drugs and electroporation, characterized in that it is formed by at least two parts, wherein a first part (122) comprises triggering components and controllers and a second part (123) comprises actuator and applicator components. 4. The applicator device (121) according to claim 3, characterized in that the first part (122) corresponds to a sealed part of the applicator device (121), which is formed by at least one controller component (124) and at least one pump system (125), and the second part (123) corresponds to the part in which a syringe (15) containing the drug to be applied to the patient is inserted, the second part (123) consisting of a first base portion (127) and a second cap portion (128) (128). 5. The applicator device (121) according to claim 4, characterized in that the first base portion (127) has support points and movement of the syringe (15), containing a first flange (130) for fastening and moving a syringe plunger (151) and a second flange (131) for fastening a syringe body (152), the second flange (131) being able to move linearly towards the first flange (130), the first flange (130) and the second flange (131) each having a sensor (S1 , S2) of position. 6. The applicator device (121) according to claim 5, characterized in that at the end of the first base portion (127) a support wedge (129) is provided, giving the necessary support to maintain an injection needle (154) correctly positioned during a drug application procedure, in which two electrical terminals (T1 , T2) are embedded inside the support wedge (129), being electrically associated with the controller component (124) and the pump system (125). 7. A method of preparing the applicator device (121) of drugs and electroporation, as defined in claims 3 to 6, characterized in that it comprises the steps of: i) opening a second cap portion (128) of the applicator device (121); ii) fitting a syringe (15), containing drug and equipped with an injection needle (154), in a first base portion (127) of the applicator device (121); iii) fitting an electroporation applicator tip, provided with at least one electroporation needle, on the injection needle (154); iv) establishment of electrical contact between the electroporation applicator tip and electrical terminals (T 1 , T2) provided at one end of the first base portion (127) of the applicator device (121); v) closing of the second cap portion (128) of the applicator device (121). 8. A method of calculating and adjusting an electric current for electroporation, characterized in that it comprises the steps of: a) activating a trigger device (113) that activates an electrical pulse generator (11 1); b) emitting at least one previously calibrated electrical pulse, by way of the electrical pulse generator (111), up to at least one electroporation needle provided in an applicator device (121) of drugs and electroporation inserted in a region of a living tissue; 

 c) real-time gauging of electric current propagated by living tissue by means of the applicator device (121); d) real-time calculation of electrical resistance of living tissue by the electrical pulse generator (111), specifically in the insertion region of the applicator device (121); e) real-time adjustment of electric current supplied by the electric pulse generator (11 1) by means of a calibrator module (112).
